---
title: "Plot TRrate - ETREF "
author: "Laura Gregoire"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  pdf_document:
    latex_engine: xelatex
---
Goal : represent one genotype across various platform, plot TR- etref in each context
```{r prepare lysi data}
rm(list = ls())
library(ggrepel)
library(dplyr)
library(segmented)
 i= "37" #N_GENO CERAAS IS11119


opPATH.obj= "./saved_objects/"
  if (!dir.exists(opPATH.obj)) dir.create(opPATH.obj, recursive = TRUE)


# Lysimeter
trial = "LYSI_SG_2024"
formula_name = "linear" 
Xvar = "ETREF_KC"
YVvar= "TR"  

load( file = paste0(opPATH.obj, "cleaned_data_TR_ETREF_",trial,".RData"))



```

#### plot  lysi

```{r plot models TR- ETREF_KC }

# 
# path = paste0("./figures/", trial, "/")
# 
# opPATH.plot_TR= path
#   if (!dir.exists(opPATH.plot_TR)) dir.create(opPATH.plot_TR, recursive = TRUE)

data$N_GENO = as.factor(data$N_GENO)
data$REPETITION = as.factor(data$REPETITION)


# for (i in levels(data$N_GENO)) {

subset <- data %>% filter(N_GENO == i)
x_min <- 0
  x_max <- max(subset$ETREF_KC, na.rm = TRUE) * 1.1  ## to have the same x axis 
  x_breaks <- pretty(c(x_min, x_max), n = 5)
  geno_name <- subset$GENO_NAME[1]

  # Keep only repetitions with ≥ 3 complete cases
  valid_reps <- subset %>%
    group_by(REPETITION) %>%
    filter(sum(!is.na(TR_MM_DAY) & !is.na(ETREF_KC)) >= 3) %>%
    pull(REPETITION) %>%
    unique()

  subset <- subset %>% filter(REPETITION %in% valid_reps)
  subset$REPETITION <- factor(subset$REPETITION, levels = c("1","2","3","4"))


  ### -----------------------
  ### 1. Linear model (intercept = 0)
  ### -----------------------
  
    if ((length(valid_reps) > 0) == TRUE) {

        # prep labels
      
      
      label_lin <- subset %>%
  group_by(REPETITION) %>%
  reframe({
    mod <- lm(TR_MM_DAY ~ 0 + ETREF_KC, data = cur_data())
    slope <- as.numeric(coef(mod)[["ETREF_KC"]])
    r2_val <- summary(mod)$r.squared
    eq <- paste0("y = ", format(slope, scientific = TRUE, digits = 3), " * x")

    tibble(REPETITION = cur_group()$REPETITION[[1]],
           slope = slope,
           intercept = 0,
           eq = eq,
           r2 = round(r2_val, 2))
  }) %>%
  arrange(as.numeric(REPETITION))

y_top <- max(subset$TR_MM_DAY) * 1.05
y_bottom <- y_top - 0.2 * (y_top - min(subset$TR_MM_DAY))

label_lin <- label_lin %>%
  mutate(
    x_label = min(subset$ETREF_KC) * 0.02,
    y_label = seq(from = y_top, to = y_bottom, length.out = n()),
    eq_r2   = paste0("Rep ", REPETITION, ": ", eq, "  R² = ", r2)
  )


# -----------------------
# Graphique : points, régression, droite prolongée, labels fixes en haut à gauche
# -----------------------
plot_lin_lysi <- ggplot(subset, aes(x = ETREF_KC, y = TR_MM_DAY, color = REPETITION)) +
  geom_point(size = 1) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", formula = y ~ 0 + x, se = FALSE, linewidth = 1) +
  geom_abline(
    data = label_lin,
    aes(slope = slope, intercept = intercept, color = REPETITION),
    linetype = "dashed",
    linewidth = 0.8,
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  geom_text(
    data = label_lin,
    aes(x = x_label, y = y_label, label = eq_r2, color = REPETITION),
    hjust = 0,        # aligné à gauche
    vjust = 1,        # aligné vers le haut
    size = 3, ## agranir le txt
    lineheight = 1.1,
    show.legend = FALSE,
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    limits = c(x_min, x_max),
    breaks = x_breaks
  ) +
#  scale_color_manual(values = rep_colors) +
  labs(
  x = expression(ET[ref] %*% K[c] ~ (mm %.% day^{-1})),
  y = "Tr (mm·day⁻¹)",
    color = "Repetition"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 9)

    }
  
  
  
  # ### -----------------------
#     ### 2. Segmented (breakpoint) model
#  # ---------------------------
 all_bp_data <- data.frame()
 annotations <- data.frame(REPETITION = factor(),
                         label = character(),
                           x = numeric(),
                           y = numeric(),
                           stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
 
 # ---------------------------
# # Boucle sur chaque répétition
# # ---------------------------

  for (rep in unique(subset$REPETITION)) {
  subset_rep <- subset %>% filter(REPETITION == rep)
  subset_rep$ETREF_KC <- as.numeric(subset_rep$ETREF_KC)

 
  bp_model <- tryCatch({
    lm_base <- lm(TR_MM_DAY ~ ETREF_KC, data = subset_rep)
    segmented(lm_base, seg.Z = ~ ETREF_KC,
              psi = list(ETREF_KC = median(subset_rep$ETREF_KC, na.rm = TRUE)))
  }, error = function(e) NULL)
  
  
  if (!is.null(bp_model)) {
#     # Générer les prédictions
    bp_data <- tryCatch({
      data.frame(ETREF_KC = seq(min(subset_rep$ETREF_KC),
                                max(subset_rep$ETREF_KC),
                                length.out = 100)) %>%
        mutate(TR_MM_DAY = predict(bp_model, newdata = .),
               REPETITION = rep)
    }, error = function(e) NULL)

    if (!is.null(bp_data)) all_bp_data <- rbind(all_bp_data, bp_data)

  #  Récupérer les pentes et breakpoint
    slope_vals <- tryCatch(slope(bp_model)$ETREF_KC, error = function(e) NULL)
    if (!is.null(slope_vals) && length(slope_vals) >= 2) {
      slope1 <- format(slope_vals[1], scientific = TRUE, digits = 3)
      slope2 <- format(slope_vals[2], scientific = TRUE, digits = 3)
      psi <- format(bp_model$psi[2], scientific = FALSE, digits = 3)
      r2_bp <- round(summary(bp_model)$r.squared, 2)

     # Ajouter annotation
      annotations <- rbind(annotations, data.frame(
        REPETITION = rep,
        label = paste0("S1=", slope1, ", S2=", slope2,
                       ", Psi=", psi, ", R²=", r2_bp),
        stringsAsFactors = FALSE
      ))
    }
  }
}
     
# ---------------------------
# # Harmoniser les facteurs et espacer verticalement
# # ---------------------------
annotations$REPETITION <- factor(
  annotations$REPETITION,
  levels = c("1","2","3","4")
)
 
annotations <- annotations %>%
  mutate(
    x = 0,
    y = case_when(
      REPETITION == "1" ~ max(subset$TR_MM_DAY)*1.02,
      REPETITION == "2" ~ max(subset$TR_MM_DAY)*0.97,
      REPETITION == "3" ~ max(subset$TR_MM_DAY)*0.92,
      REPETITION == "4" ~ max(subset$TR_MM_DAY)*0.87
    )
  )


# 
# n_rep <- nrow(annotations)
# annotations <- annotations %>%
#   mutate(
#     x = x_max*1.03,
#     y = seq(max(subset$TR_MM_DAY, na.rm=TRUE),
#             max(subset$TR_MM_DAY, na.rm=TRUE)*0.7,
#             length.out=n())
#   )





plot_segm_lysi <- ggplot(subset, aes(x = ETREF_KC, y = TR_MM_DAY, color = REPETITION)) +
  geom_point(size = 1) +

  geom_line(
    data = all_bp_data,
    aes(x = ETREF_KC, y = TR_MM_DAY, group = REPETITION, color = REPETITION),
    size = 1
  ) +
geom_text(
  data = annotations,
  aes(x = x, y = y, label = label, color = REPETITION),
  hjust = 0,
  size = 3,
  show.legend = FALSE
) +

#  scale_color_manual(values = rep_colors) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = x_breaks) +

  coord_cartesian(
    xlim = c(x_min, x_max),
    clip = "off"
  ) +

  labs(
  x = expression(ET[ref] %*% K[c] ~ (mm %.% day^{-1})),
    y = "Tr (mm·day⁻¹)",
    color = "Repetition"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 9) +
  theme(
    plot.margin = margin(5.5, 60, 5.5, 5.5)   # space for annotations
  )

  


```



#### plot LTP

```{r prepare LTP data}
# LTP
i="37"
trial = "LTP_SG_2024"
formula_name = "linear" 
Xvar = "ETREF"
YVvar= "TRrate"  



opPATH.obj= "./saved_objects/"
  if (!dir.exists(opPATH.obj)) dir.create(opPATH.obj, recursive = TRUE)


load( file = paste0(opPATH.obj, "cleaned_data_TR_ETREF_",trial,".RData"))

```


```{r plot  TRrate- ETREF}


data$N_GENO = as.factor(data$N_GENO)


# for (i in levels(data$N_GENO)) {

  subset <- data %>% filter(N_GENO == i)
  x_min <- 0
  x_max <- max(subset$ETREF, na.rm = TRUE) * 1.1  ## to have the same x axis 
  geno_name <- subset$GENO_NAME[1]
  
  

  # Keep only repetitions with ≥ 3 complete cases
  valid_reps <- subset %>%
    group_by(REPETITION) %>%
    filter(sum(!is.na(TR_RATE) & !is.na(ETREF)) >= 3) %>%
    pull(REPETITION) %>%
    unique()

  subset <- subset %>% filter(REPETITION %in% valid_reps)
  subset$REPETITION <- factor(subset$REPETITION, levels = c("1","2","3","4"))


  
  
  ### -----------------------
  ### 1. Linear model (intercept = 0)
  ### -----------------------
  
    if ((length(valid_reps) > 0) == TRUE) {

        # prep labels
label_lin <- subset %>%
  group_by(REPETITION) %>%
  reframe({
    mod <- lm(TR_RATE ~ 0 + ETREF, data = cur_data())
    slope <- as.numeric(coef(mod)[["ETREF"]])
    r2_val <- summary(mod)$r.squared

    eq <- paste0("y = ", format(slope, scientific = TRUE, digits = 3), " * x")
    
     

    tibble(
      REPETITION = cur_group()$REPETITION[[1]],
      slope = slope,
      intercept = 0,
      eq = eq,
      r2 = round(r2_val, 2)
    )
  }) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  arrange(REPETITION)

# Nombre de répétitions
n_rep <- nrow(label_lin)

# Limites verticales pour espacement
y_top <- max(subset$TR_RATE) * 1.05
y_bottom <- y_top - 0.2 * (y_top - min(subset$TR_RATE))

# Ajouter positions x et y pour les labels + texte combiné
label_lin <- label_lin %>%
  mutate(
    x_label = min(subset$ETREF) * 0.02,              # aligné à gauche
    y_label = seq(from = y_top, to = y_bottom, length.out = n()),
    eq_r2   = paste0("Rep ", REPETITION, ": ", eq, "  R² = ", r2)
  )




# ### Graphique : points, régression, droite prolongée, labels fixes en haut à gauche
plot_lin_ltp <- ggplot(subset, aes(x = ETREF, y = TR_RATE, color = REPETITION)) +
geom_point(size= 1) +
# régression locale
geom_smooth(method = "lm", formula = y ~ 0 + x, se = FALSE, linewidth = 1) +
# 
 geom_abline(data = label_lin,
              aes(slope = slope, intercept = intercept, color = REPETITION),
              linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.8, inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  geom_text(data = label_lin,
            aes(x = x_label, y = y_label, label = eq_r2, color = REPETITION),
            hjust = 0, vjust = 1, size = 3, show.legend = FALSE,
            inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    limits = c(x_min, x_max)) +

# 
#scale_color_manual(values = rep_colors) +
labs(
    x = expression(ET[ref] ~ (mm %.% h^{-1})),
  y = "TR (g·h⁻¹·cm⁻²)",
  color = "Repetition"
) +
theme_bw(base_size = 9) +
# 
# axe x commence à 0
coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, NA))
}


  # ### -----------------------
#     ### 2. Segmented (breakpoint) model
#  # ---------------------------
 all_bp_data <- data.frame()
 annotations <- data.frame(REPETITION = factor(),
                         label = character(),
                           x = numeric(),
                           y = numeric(),
                           stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
 
 
 
 
 # ---------------------------
# # Boucle sur chaque répétition
# # ---------------------------

  for (rep in unique(subset$REPETITION)) {
  subset_rep <- subset %>% filter(REPETITION == rep)
  subset_rep$ETREF <- as.numeric(subset_rep$ETREF)

 
  bp_model <- tryCatch({
    lm_base <- lm(TR_RATE ~ ETREF, data = subset_rep)
    segmented(lm_base, seg.Z = ~ ETREF,
              psi = list(ETREF = median(subset_rep$ETREF, na.rm = TRUE)))
  }, error = function(e) NULL)
  
  
  if (!is.null(bp_model)) {
#     # Générer les prédictions
    bp_data <- tryCatch({
      data.frame(ETREF = seq(min(subset_rep$ETREF),
                                max(subset_rep$ETREF),
                                length.out = 100)) %>%
        mutate(TR_RATE = predict(bp_model, newdata = .),
               REPETITION = rep)
    }, error = function(e) NULL)

    if (!is.null(bp_data)) all_bp_data <- rbind(all_bp_data, bp_data)

  #  Récupérer les pentes et breakpoint
    slope_vals <- tryCatch(slope(bp_model)$ETREF, error = function(e) NULL)
    if (!is.null(slope_vals) && length(slope_vals) >= 2) {
      slope1 <- format(slope_vals[1], scientific = TRUE, digits = 3)
      slope2 <- format(slope_vals[2], scientific = TRUE, digits = 3)
      psi <- format(bp_model$psi[2], scientific = FALSE, digits = 2)
      r2_bp <- round(summary(bp_model)$r.squared, 2)

     # Ajouter annotation
      annotations <- rbind(annotations, data.frame(
        REPETITION = rep,
        label = paste0("S1=", slope1, ", S2=", slope2,
                       ", Psi=", psi, ", R²=", r2_bp),
        stringsAsFactors = FALSE
      ))
    }
  }
}
     
# ---------------------------
# # Harmoniser les facteurs et espacer verticalement
# # ---------------------------
 
 annotations$REPETITION <- factor(
  annotations$REPETITION,
  levels = c("1","2","3","4")
)
 
annotations <- annotations %>%
  mutate(
    x = -0.001,
    y = case_when(
      REPETITION == "1" ~ max(subset$TR_RATE)*1.02,
      REPETITION == "2" ~ max(subset$TR_RATE)*0.97,
      REPETITION == "3" ~ max(subset$TR_RATE)*0.92,
      REPETITION == "4" ~ max(subset$TR_RATE)*0.87
    )
  )


plot_segm_ltp<- ggplot(subset, aes(x = ETREF, y = TR_RATE, color = REPETITION)) +
  geom_point(size = 1) +
  geom_line(
    data = all_bp_data,
    aes(x = ETREF, y = TR_RATE, group = REPETITION, color = REPETITION),
    size = 1
  ) +
  geom_text(
  data = annotations,
  aes(x = x, y = y, label = label, color = REPETITION),
  hjust = 0,
  size = 3,
  show.legend = FALSE
) +

  # geom_text_repel(
  #   data = annotations,
  #   inherit.aes = FALSE,
  #   aes(x = x, y = y, label = label, color = REPETITION),
  #   direction = "y",
  #   hjust = 0,
  #       size = 3,
  #   box.padding = 0.2,
  #   max.overlaps = Inf,
  #   segment.color = NA,
  #   show.legend = FALSE
  # ) +

#  scale_color_manual(values = rep_colors) +
  # scale_x_continuous(breaks = x_breaks) +

  coord_cartesian(
    xlim = c(x_min, x_max),
    clip = "off"
  ) +

  labs(
    x = expression(ET[ref] ~ (mm %.% h^{-1})),
  y = "TR (g·h⁻¹·cm⁻²)",
    color = "Repetition"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 9) +
  theme(
    plot.margin = margin(5.5, 60, 5.5, 5.5)   # space for annotations
  )

```




#### plot HTP


```{r}
# HTP

## N_GENO ICRISAT different from CERAAS for a same GENO_NAME
 i= "89" #N_GENO ICRISAT IS11119
trial= "HTP_SG_2023"
formula_name = "linear" 
Xvar = "ETREF"
YVvar= "TR_MM_H"  

load( file = paste0(opPATH.obj, "cleaned_data_TR_ETREF_",trial,".RData"))


```

```{r plot  TR- ETREF }

  
data$N_GENO = as.factor(data$N_GENO)

# for (i in levels(data$N_GENO)) {

  subset <- data %>% filter(N_GENO == i)
  x_min <- 0
  x_max <- max(subset$ETREF, na.rm = TRUE) * 1.1  ## to have the same x axis 
  x_breaks <- pretty(c(x_min, x_max), n = 5)
  geno_name <- subset$GENO_NAME[1]

  # Keep only repetitions with ≥ 3 complete cases
  valid_reps <- subset %>%
    group_by(REPETITION) %>%
    filter(sum(!is.na(TR_MM_H) & !is.na(ETREF)) >= 3) %>%
    pull(REPETITION) %>%
    unique()

  subset <- subset %>% filter(REPETITION %in% valid_reps)
  subset$REPETITION <- factor(subset$REPETITION, levels = c("1","2","3","4"))


  ### -----------------------
  ### 1. Linear model (intercept = 0)
  ### -----------------------
  

        # prep labels
label_lin <- subset %>%
  group_by(REPETITION) %>%
  reframe({
    mod <- lm(TR_MM_H ~ 0 + ETREF, data = cur_data())
    slope <- as.numeric(coef(mod)[["ETREF"]])
    r2_val <- summary(mod)$r.squared

    eq <- paste0("y = ", format(slope, scientific = FALSE, digits = 3), " * x")
    
    tibble(
      REPETITION = cur_group()$REPETITION[[1]],
      slope = slope,
      intercept = 0,
      eq = eq,
      r2 = round(r2_val, 2)
    )
  }) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  arrange(REPETITION)

# Nombre de répétitions
n_rep <- nrow(label_lin)

# Limites verticales pour espacement
y_top <- max(subset$TR_MM_H) * 1.05
y_bottom <- y_top - 0.2 * (y_top - min(subset$TR_MM_H))

# Ajouter positions x et y pour les labels + texte combiné
label_lin <- label_lin %>%
  mutate(
    x_label = min(subset$ETREF_KC) * 0.02,
    y_label = seq(from = y_top, to = y_bottom, length.out = n()),
    eq_r2   = paste0("Rep ", REPETITION, ": ", eq, "  R² = ", r2)
  )

  ### -----------------------
  ### 1. Linear model (intercept = 0)
  ### -----------------------

######
# ### Graphique : points, régression, droite prolongée, labels fixes en haut à gauche
plot_lin_htp <- ggplot(subset, aes(x = ETREF, y = TR_MM_H, color = REPETITION)) +
geom_point(size= 1) +
# 
# régression locale
geom_smooth(method = "lm", formula = y ~ 0 + x, se = FALSE, linewidth = 1) +
# 
  geom_abline(data = label_lin,
              aes(slope = slope, intercept = intercept, color = REPETITION),
              linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.8, inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  geom_text(data = label_lin,
            aes(x = x_label, y = y_label, label = eq_r2, color = REPETITION),
            hjust = 0, vjust = 1, size = 3, show.legend = FALSE,
            inherit.aes = FALSE) +
# 
# scale_color_manual(values = rep_colors) +
labs(
      x = expression(ET[ref] ~ (mm %.% h^{-1})),

  y = "Tr (mm·h⁻¹)",
  color = "Repetition"
) +
theme_bw(base_size = 9) +
# 
# axe x commence à 0
coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, NA))


  # ### -----------------------
#     ### 2. Segmented (breakpoint) model
#  # ---------------------------
 all_bp_data <- data.frame()
 annotations <- data.frame(REPETITION = factor(),
                         label = character(),
                           x = numeric(),
                           y = numeric(),
                           stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
 
 
 
 
 # ---------------------------
# # Boucle sur chaque répétition
# # ---------------------------

  for (rep in unique(subset$REPETITION)) {
  subset_rep <- subset %>% filter(REPETITION == rep)
  subset_rep$ETREF <- as.numeric(subset_rep$ETREF)

 
  bp_model <- tryCatch({
    lm_base <- lm(TR_MM_H ~ ETREF, data = subset_rep)
    segmented(lm_base, seg.Z = ~ ETREF,
              psi = list(ETREF = median(subset_rep$ETREF, na.rm = TRUE)))
  }, error = function(e) NULL)
  
  
  if (!is.null(bp_model)) {
#     # Générer les prédictions
    bp_data <- tryCatch({
      data.frame(ETREF = seq(min(subset_rep$ETREF),
                                max(subset_rep$ETREF),
                                length.out = 100)) %>%
        mutate(TR_MM_H = predict(bp_model, newdata = .),
               REPETITION = rep)
    }, error = function(e) NULL)

    if (!is.null(bp_data)) all_bp_data <- rbind(all_bp_data, bp_data)

  #  Récupérer les pentes et breakpoint
    slope_vals <- tryCatch(slope(bp_model)$ETREF, error = function(e) NULL)
    if (!is.null(slope_vals) && length(slope_vals) >= 2) {
      slope1 <- format(slope_vals[1], scientific = FALSE, digits = 2)
      slope2 <- format(slope_vals[2], scientific = FALSE, digits = 2)
      psi <- format(bp_model$psi[2], scientific = FALSE, digits = 2)
      r2_bp <- round(summary(bp_model)$r.squared, 2)

     # Ajouter annotation
      annotations <- rbind(annotations, data.frame(
        REPETITION = rep,
        label = paste0("S1=", slope1, ", S2=", slope2,
                       ", Psi=", psi, ", R²=", r2_bp),
        stringsAsFactors = FALSE
      ))
    }
  }
}
     
# ---------------------------
# # Harmoniser les facteurs et espacer verticalement
# # ---------------------------
 
 annotations$REPETITION <- factor(
  annotations$REPETITION,
  levels = c("1","2","3","4")
)
 
annotations <- annotations %>%
  mutate(
    x = -0.001,
    y = case_when(
      REPETITION == "1" ~ max(subset$TR_MM_H)*1.02,
      REPETITION == "2" ~ max(subset$TR_MM_H)*0.97,
      REPETITION == "3" ~ max(subset$TR_MM_H)*0.92,
      REPETITION == "4" ~ max(subset$TR_MM_H)*0.87
    )
  )

annotations <- annotations %>%
  arrange(as.numeric(REPETITION))


plot_segm_htp<- ggplot(subset, aes(x = ETREF, y = TR_MM_H, color = REPETITION)) +
  geom_point(size = 1) +
  geom_line(
    data = all_bp_data,
    aes(x = ETREF, y = TR_MM_H, group = REPETITION, color = REPETITION),
    size = 1
  ) +
  geom_text(
  data = annotations,
  aes(x = x, y = y, label = label, color = REPETITION),
  hjust = 0,
  size = 3,
  show.legend = FALSE
) +

  # geom_text_repel(
  #   data = annotations,
  #   inherit.aes = FALSE,
  #   aes(x = x, y = y, label = label, color = REPETITION),
  #   direction = "y",
  #   hjust = 0,
  #       size = 3,
  #   box.padding = 0.2,
  #   max.overlaps = Inf,
  #   segment.color = NA,
  #   show.legend = FALSE
  # ) +

#  scale_color_manual(values = rep_colors) +
  # scale_x_continuous(breaks = x_breaks) +

  coord_cartesian(
    xlim = c(x_min, x_max),
    clip = "off"
  ) +

  labs(
    x = expression(ET[ref] ~ (mm %.% h^{-1})),
  y = "Tr (mm·h⁻¹)",
    color = "Repetition"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 9) +
  theme(
    plot.margin = margin(5.5, 60, 5.5, 5.5)   # space for annotations
  )

```




```{r}
library(patchwork)
library(ggplot2)

library(patchwork)
library(ggplot2)

# Définir le thème publication
theme_publi <- theme(
  axis.title = element_text(size = 9),
  axis.text  = element_text(size = 9),
  legend.title = element_text(size = 9),
  legend.text  = element_text(size = 9),
  plot.title   = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
  legend.position = "bottom"
)

# Définir la scale couleur unique pour tous les plots
rep_colors <- c("1" = "#A65E00", "2" = "#1C64A6", "3" = "#006747", "4" = "#7A2E00")
scale_rep <- scale_color_manual(values = rep_colors, name = "Repetition")

plot_lin_ltp  <- plot_lin_ltp  + scale_rep
plot_segm_ltp <- plot_segm_ltp + scale_rep
plot_lin_htp  <- plot_lin_htp  + scale_rep
plot_segm_htp <- plot_segm_htp + scale_rep
plot_lin_lysi <- plot_lin_lysi + scale_rep
plot_segm_lysi <- plot_segm_lysi + scale_rep


# Assembler les plots avec patchwork et une seule légende
fig_finale <-
(
    (plot_lin_ltp  | plot_segm_ltp)  /
  (plot_lin_htp  | plot_segm_htp) /
  (plot_lin_lysi | plot_segm_lysi)

) +
  plot_layout(guides = "collect") &
  plot_annotation(tag_levels = "A") &   # <-- lettres A, B, C...
  theme_publi

# Enregistrer la figure
ggsave(
  filename = "./figures/plot_TR_ETREF_across_platforms.png",
  plot     = fig_finale,
  width    = 20,
  height   = 25,
  units    = "cm",
  dpi      = 300
)


```
